subject: Mathematics
title: Galois: Genius Cut Short
description: A micro-lesson that makes Galois: Genius Cut Short usable.

hook.text: The night before a duel, a 20-year-old scribbled math notes in the margin—and changed algebra forever.

buildup.text: Evariste Galois was rejected twice from the top university and jailed for political activism.

discovery.text: His theory of groups explained exactly when polynomial equations can be solved by formula.

twist.text: He died in 1832 from wounds in a duel, probably over a romantic dispute. He was 20 years old.

climax.text: His hastily written notes became the foundation of modern abstract algebra.

punchline.text: Twenty years of life and one night of scribbling changed algebra.

quiz.question: What field did Galois essentially create?
